arts.ttl: Newcastle’s Sandro Tonali Eyeing Summer Exit Amid Juventus, PSG Interest
Newcastle see Johan Bakayoko transfer opportunity as Marc Guehi similarity clear
arts.sum: Crucially, away from the pitch, the player's contract currently runs out in 2026, meaning the January window could be the last chance for PSV to cash in before his market value plummets as he enters the final months of his deal. PSV, according to well-placed sources in the Netherlands, have rather overambitious hopes of holding out for a big fee for Bakayoko's services next month, while Newcastle are looking for a knockdown price given the player's contract situation and the fact they remain in a tricky position, financially, themselves heading into next month's window.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United star reveals his one transfer wish – Magpies fans can rest easy for now
arts.sum: Newcastle United midfielder Bruno Guimarães has revealed he wants to play for Vasco in future, despite starting his career with Athletico. O Globo cover comments from the Newcastle United star today, made to EPSN, in which he reveals playing for Vasco would be the fulfilment of his father’s dreams. arts.ttl: Man United, Newcastle 'handed boost' in pursuit of Premier League wonderkid
arts.sum: Former Manchester United chief scout Mick Brown says that Southampton would almost certainly be forced to sell Tyler Dibling if they are relegated from the Premier League.

arts.ttl: 'My body's breaking' - Ex-Palace & Newcastle striker Gayle to retire
arts.sum: Hibernian forward Dwight Gayle says his "body is breaking down" and he intends to retire as a player at the end of this season.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United players get mentality change order after team meeting
arts.sum: Newcastle United star Dan Burn has ordered a mentality switch ahead of the long haul trip to Ipswich Town. Burn told Chronicle Live: "It has been a long time since Newcastle have been down to Ipswich.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United make 'respect' promise to star with January transfer talks now active
arts.sum: The 30-year-old is one of the favourites to leave Newcastle this January and as Chronicle Live reported last night, talks between the club and Almiron's representatives are active as far as a possible departure is concerned for the South American. Newcastle United boss Eddie Howe says he will show Miguel Almiron the "respect he deserves" as long as he's at St James' Park.
